title: Duro Wait To Be Pampered
age_suitability: play that weaves mystery, love, and destiny into a rich tapestry of African spirituality. When an Abiku, a spirit-child who has journeyed between worlds thirteen times, decides to stay on earth with her mother, her otherworldly spouse returns to lure her back for a once-in-a-century festival. Torn between eternal love and earthly devotion, Duro faces a heart-wrenching choice that blurs the line between life and the afterlife. Set in a world where music, myth, and politics intertwine, *DURO!* captivates with its poetry, symbolism, and emotional power — a timeless reflection on love, sacrifice, and the spirit’s longing to belong.

title: Face It Till You Make It The Panic Years
company: Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the relentless expectations of social media. From the awkward intimacy of smear tests to the clash between generations, and the chaos of adulthood no one warned us about, this show celebrates imperfection and speaks the truths we all think but rarely dare to say out loud. A love letter and a reality check for anyone learning to survive adulthood one questionable decision at a time.

title: Saving Myself
company: books, half-remembered rituals, and an unlit candle. Since childhood, Lilly has been blamed for things going wrong: pets that disappeared, toys that broke, the uneasy silences that followed her through her family home. Over time, these stories became fact, shaping how others treated her and how she learned to understand herself.

Raw, darkly comic, and unsettling, SAVING MYSELF asks what happens when care is replaced by superstition, and what it costs a woman to save herself.
